# Learnings

- Product specs require habits to appear in alphabetical order by name. Persisted manual ordering is not part of the accepted UX, so `DisplayOrder` and reorder flows should not be reintroduced unless the specs change.
- Checkin records are presence-only: a `Checkins` row should exist only for completed days, and same-day uncheck should delete the row rather than persist a false state.
- Habit deletion is confirmed through a `MudDialog` on the Habit Details page, while the page itself owns the `IHabitService.DeleteAsync(...)` call, redirects to home on success, and relies on SQLite cascade delete to remove related `Checkins`.
- Habit editing now uses a separate `MudDialog` form from the Habit Details page; the dialog performs `IHabitService.UpdateAsync(...)`, then the page reloads in place so the user stays on Habit Details.
- The core unit test project now lives under `tests\Streak.Core.UnitTests` to mirror `src\Streak.Core`, and individual unit test files should group scenarios with `#region Positive tests`, `#region Negative tests`, and `#region Boundary tests` where applicable.
- Habit details now compose streak/history through `Components\Cards\HabitStreakHistoryCard.razor`, while `HabitDetails.razor` stays focused on loading/orchestration and `HabitHistoryHeatmapBuilder` in `Streak.Core` projects raw checkins into the heatmap view model.
- `MainLayout.razor` now supplies a custom MudBlazor theme so the app's semantic colors match the specs: `Secondary` is teal for history accents, `Success` is green for checked-in state, and muted helper text should use `--mud-palette-text-secondary` rather than `Color.Secondary`.
- View model types under `src\Streak.Core\Models\ViewModels\` should use `#region Display Properties`, `#region Computed Properties`, and `#region Hidden Properties` to separate UI-bound values from derived/internal ones; input models may use only the regions that actually apply.
- The deploy workflow should publish Android as an arm64-only APK using `-r android-arm64 -p:AndroidPackageFormat=apk`, then upload the generated `*-Signed.apk` as an artifact so the CI output stays sideloadable for personal devices without inflating the APK with emulator/x86 runtimes.
- The app-wide habit cap is centralized in `src\Streak.Core\Constants\CoreConstants.cs` as `CoreConstants.MaxHabitCount` (currently 10); create-flow enforcement and homepage CTA visibility should use that shared constant instead of duplicating numbers.
- In Azure Functions isolated worker with ASP.NET Core integration, `IActionResult`, `HttpRequest`, and ASP.NET-style result types are supported, but ASP.NET Core routing is not; Function routes must still live on `[HttpTrigger(..., Route = ...)]`, even when endpoint classes derive from `NControllerBase`.
- Settings export is platform-specific by design: Windows should use a native `FileSavePicker` and treat dialog cancellation as a non-error, while Android should write the backup into `MediaStore.Downloads` instead of opening a share sheet.
- The current Settings UI still omits the reminder section, but the data card now includes a UI-only `Daily automated backups` subsection ahead of `Backup` and `Restore`; keep it concise with a short description, a small info tooltip, and a standalone non-functional switch for now.
- Settings restore now mirrors the specs within the existing data card: `Upload DB` uses a native file picker plus confirmation dialog, validates the selected SQLite file by integrity/schema before replacing the live DB, and rolls back to the previous database artifacts if restore fails.
- SQLite startup still bootstraps from `restore-db.sql` only for missing databases, so persisted schema changes must ship with an explicit startup/import upgrade step for existing installs instead of relying on EF migrations.
- The `Checkins` schema now includes an optional `Notes` column capped at 50 characters; fresh databases get the constraint from `restore-db.sql`, while existing installs are upgraded in `SqliteDatabaseSchemaUpgrader` by rebuilding `Checkins` in place so the new constraint and nullable column both land without losing history.
- Windows restore should stay explicit about platform UX during local dev: use a native `FileOpenPicker` filtered to `.db` files on Windows, while Android can keep using the system picker abstraction for backup selection.
- DB sharing is intentionally Android-only for now: Android 13 should use the native sharesheet with a generated cache-backed `.db` backup, while the Windows `Share DB` button stays disabled and local dev should rely on `Download DB` instead.
- The Settings data card spec/mockup now keeps Backup and Restore visually symmetric by moving the save-location help and destructive restore warning into small tooltip icons next to each subsection heading, rather than showing mismatched inline note blocks.
- The Settings data card should keep the Daily automated backups subsection visually matched with Backup and Restore: no schedule chip or inline toggle copy, just a short description, a concise info tooltip, and a standalone trailing switch.
- In the Settings data card mockup/spec, the Backup and Restore buttons should stay visually matched as a pair, using the same filled styling and directional download/upload icons rather than mixed filled/outline treatments.
- The shared `SqlGenericRepositoryBase` should detach entities after successful add/update writes, and detach any already-tracked entity with the same primary key before updates. In the MAUI app, the `StreakDbContext` can live long enough for repeat dialog submits to hit EF Core's "another instance with the same key is already being tracked" error otherwise.
- Streak's day-boundary semantics are intentionally local-time-first: check-in toggles, streak calculations, date banners, and heatmap "today" state should all use the device's current local calendar day, with the accepted tradeoff that timezone travel or manual clock changes can shift what counts as "today."
- Automated backup infrastructure is Android-only for now: the enabled flag is persisted in local SQLite, the Settings toggle becomes the source of truth, and both app startup and Android receivers honor that stored state before scheduling or cancelling the fixed 11:30 PM `AlarmManager` trigger.
- Automated daily backups now execute on Android by creating a standalone `streak-auto-backup-YYYYMMdd-HHmmss.db` copy and saving it into `Downloads/Streak`; on Windows the Settings toggle stays disabled because the feature is unavailable there.
- In the MAUI Blazor Hybrid Android host, the app shell must stay inside the platform safe drawing area; calling `WindowCompat.SetDecorFitsSystemWindows(Window, true)` in `MainActivity` prevents the Blazor app bar and bottom homepage CTA from rendering under Android system bars, while `dvh` is safer than raw `vh` for full-height web content.
- Diagnostics and telemetry are now specified as local-first requirements: app logging should flow through `ILogger`, persist to app-private structured log files, and be retrieved for support through explicit Settings export/share actions rather than any mandatory cloud telemetry service or manual sandbox browsing.
- The MAUI host now uses Serilog as the backing provider for `ILogger`, writing compact structured events into one app-private `Diagnostics\streak-diagnostics.log` file capped at 5 MB by a custom circular sink; Android automated-backup receivers resolve their loggers and services from MAUI DI so background execution lands in the same file.
- The Settings page spec and mockup now treat diagnostic export as its own sibling maintenance action within the Data card: a dedicated **Diagnostic logs** subsection sits alongside Backup and Restore, exports a timestamped `.zip` support bundle, and explicitly excludes the full database unless the user separately chooses DB backup/share.
- Diagnostic export now packages the persistent app-private `Diagnostics` directory into a timestamped `streak-diagnostics-YYYYMMdd-HHmmss.zip` bundle, always adds a small `manifest.json`, and keeps database contents out of the support bundle.
- Homepage check-in dialogs are page-orchestrated: `Home.razor` opens the optional-note dialog before same-day check-in creation and a confirmation dialog before unchecking, while `HabitCheckin.razor` stays presentational and reports checkbox changes through an `EventCallback`.
- `HabitCheckin.razor` must keep the checkbox visually pinned to the last committed `ViewModel.IsDoneForToday` state until a dialog-backed action is confirmed, then force a keyed `MudCheckBox` re-render after each toggle attempt; otherwise the UI can both drift after cancel and prematurely show checked/unchecked state while the dialog is still open.
- Secondary-screen app-bar titles now flow through `Components\Layout\AppBarTitleScope.razor` and `AppBarTitleState.cs`: `MainLayout.razor` keeps the homepage wordmark/actions, while routed pages publish `Back + title` titles, preserving the emoji and applying one-line ellipsis only to the habit name text when space is tight. Do not clear that shared title in `HandleLocationChanged(...)`; validate it with router-based tests through `Routes.razor`, because direct `LayoutView` tests can miss the live-navigation regression where the title disappears after route changes.
